title: Sealed Packets
story: [Meridian-7 Log #013] rEx's data uses an encapsulation format, similar to self-closing container tags: starting with < and ending with />. Some are paired open/close tags (containing content), while others are self-closing (standalone data packets). You need to extract all self-closing packets — they are independent, complete units of information.
hints: [Hint] Self-closing tags start with < and end with />
[Hint] There may be spaces and content between the tag name and attributes
[Hint] There may be an optional space before />
promptHints: Describe the start and end markers of self-closing tags
Tell the AI that any attribute content can appear in the middle
Hint the AI to use grouping to organize the regex structure

feedback.onPass: [Pattern Accepted] Tag structure recognized. The signal's syntax grows ever more complex.
feedback.onFail: [Parse Failed] Tag matching failed. What is the key feature of self-closing tags?
feedback.onPerfect: [Decryption Success] Grouping perfectly applied. Even complex structures can't stump you.
feedback.onDirectWrite: [Manual Override] Manual tag parsing. Grouping is a breeze for you.
